1.1: PAUL, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, to those who are in Eph'e-sus, saints and believers in Jesus Christ:
1.2: Peace be with you and grace from God our Father, and from our LORD Jesus Christ:
1.3: Blessed be the God, and Father of our LORD Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heaven through Christ:
1.4: Just as from the beginning he has chosen us through him, before the foundation of the world, that we may become holy and without blemish before him.
1.5: And he marked us with his love to be his from the beginning, and adopted us to be sons through Jesus Christ, as it pleased his will.
1.6: To the praise of the glory of his grace that he has poured upon us by his beloved one.
1.7: In him we have salvation, and in his blood, forgiveness of sins, according to the richness of his grace;
1.8: That that grace which has abounded in us, in all wisdom and spiritual understanding;
1.9: And because he has made known to us the mystery of his will, as he has ordained from the very beginning, to work through it;
1.10: As a dispensation of the fulness of times, that all things might be made new in heaven and on earth through Christ:
1.11: By whom we have been chosen, as he had marked us from the beginning so he wanted to carry out everything according to the good judgment of his will:
1.12: That we should become the first to trust in Christ, to his honor and his glory:
1.13: In whom, you also have heard the word of truth, which is the gospel for your salvation: in him you have believed, so you are sealed with the Holy Spirit that was promised,
1.14: Which is the pledge of our inheritance, for the salvation of those who are saved, and for the glory of his honour.
1.15: Wherefore I also, since I heard of your faith in our LORD Jesus Christ, and your love toward all the saints,
1.16: Never cease to give thanks for your sakes and to mention you in my prayers;
1.17: So that the God of our LORD Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:
1.18: And so that the eyes of your understanding may be enlightened; that you may know what is the hope of his calling, and what are the glorious riches of his inheritance in the saints;
1.19: And what is the exceeding greatness of his power in us as the result of the things we believe, according to the skill of his mighty power,
1.20: Which he wrought through Christ, when he raised him from the dead and set him at his own right hand in heaven,
1.21: Far above all angels and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world but also in the world which is to come:
1.22: And has put all things under his feet, and made him, who is above all things, the head of the church,
1.23: Which is his body, and confirmation of him who fulfills all things and every thing.

2.1: AND he has quickened you also who were dead because of your sins and trespasses;
2.2: In which you previously walked according to the worldly course of this world, and according to the will of the supreme ruler of the air, the spirit which is active in the children of disobedience.
2.3: In those very deeds in which we were also corrupted from the very beginning through the lusts of the flesh, fulfilling the wills of the flesh and of the mind: thereby we became completely the children of wrath, even as others.
2.4: But God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love with which he loved us,
2.5: Even when we were dead in our sins, has made us live together with Christ, by whose grace we are saved;
2.6: And he has raised us up with him, and seated us with him in heaven, through Jesus Christ.
2.7: In the ages to come he might show the exceeding riches of his grace in his kindness toward us through Jesus Christ.
2.8: For it is by grace that you are saved through faith; not of your doing: it is the gift of God:
2.9: Not of works, lest any man should boast.
2.10: For we are his creation, created through Jesus Christ ultimately for good works, which God has before ordained that we should live in them.
2.11: Wherefore remember that you were Gentiles in the flesh from the beginning, and you were called Uncircumcision, differing from that which is called Circumcision, which is the work of the hands in the flesh.
2.12: At that time you were without Christ, being aliens to the customs of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of the promise, without hope, and without God in the world.
2.13: But now, through Jesus Christ, you who sometimes were far off are brought near by the blood of Christ.
2.14: For he is our peace, who has made both one, and has broken down the fence of separation between them;
2.15: And he has abolished by his precious body, the enmity between them, and he has abolished by his commandments, the ordinances of the law, that he may create, in his person, from the two, a new man, thus making peace;
2.16: And he reconciled both in one body with God, and with his cross he destroyed the enmity:
2.17: And he came and preached peace to you who are far away and to those who are near.
2.18: Through him we both are able to draw near by one Spirit to the Father.
2.19: Thus from henceforth you are neither strangers, nor foreigners, but fellow-citizens with the saints, and children of the household of God;
2.20: And you are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the corner-stone of the building:
2.21: And through him the whole building is fashioned and grows into a holy temple through the help of the LORD:
2.22: You also are builded by him for a habitation of God through the Spirit.

3.1: FOR this cause I, Paul, am a prisoner of Jesus Christ for the sake of you Gentiles.
3.2: Have you ever heard of the dispensation of the grace of God which was given to me for you?
3.3: For the mystery was made known to me by a revelation; as I have briefly written you before,
3.4: So that when you read it you can understand my knowledge of the mystery of Christ,
3.5: Which in ages past was not made known to the sons of men, as it is now revealed to his holy apostles and prophets by the Spirit;
3.6: That the Gentiles should be fellow heirs and partakers of his body and of the promise which is given through him by the gospel,
3.7: Of that very gospel, I have been a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God given to me by the effectual working of his power.
3.8: Even to me, who am less than the least of all the saints, this grace was given, that I should preach among the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ:
3.9: And that I may enlighten all men that they may see what is the dispensation of the mystery, which for ages had been hidden from the world by God who created all things:
3.10: To the intent that through the church the manifold wisdom of God may be made known to the angels and powers which are in heaven,
3.11: Which is the wisdom he prepared in ages past, and has carried out in Jesus Christ our LORD:
3.12: In whom we have freedom of access with confidence in his faith.
3.13: Therefore I ask that I may not grow weary in my afflictions for your sakes, which is for your happiness.
3.14: For this cause I bow my knees to the Father of our LORD Jesus Christ,
3.15: For whom all fatherhood in heaven and in earth is named,
3.16: To grant you, according to the riches of his glory, to be strengthened with might by his Spirit;
3.17: That Christ may dwell in you by faith, and in your hearts by love, strengthening your understanding and your foundation;
3.18: So that you may be able to comprehend with all the saints what is the height and depth and length and breadth;
3.19: And to know the love of Christ which surpasses all knowledge, that you may be filled with all the fulness of God.
3.20: Now to him who is able by power to do for us more than anyone else, and to do for us more than we ask or think, according to his mighty power that works in us,
3.21: Unto him be glory in his church by Jesus Christ throughout all ages, world without end: Amen.

4.1: I THEREFORE, a prisoner of our LORD, beseech you to live as is worthy of the rank to which you are called,
4.2: With all humility and gentleness and with patience, forbearing one another in love,
4.3: Endeavoring to preserve the harmony of the Spirit in the bond of peace,
4.4: That you may become one body, and one Spirit, even as you are called in one hope of your calling;
4.5: There is one LORD, one faith, and one baptism;
4.6: One God and Father of all, who is above all, and through all, and in all of us.
4.7: But to every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.
4.8: Wherefore it is said, He ascended on high, and took possession of heaven and gave good gifts to men.
4.9: Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the inner parts of the earth?
4.10: So he that descended is the same also that ascended far above all heavens, that he might fulfil all things.
4.11: And he has assigned some, apostles, and some, prophets, and some, evangelists, and some, pastors, and some, teachers;
4.12: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:
4.13: Until we all become one in faith, and in the knowledge of the Son of God, and become a perfect man according to the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:
4.14: That we henceforth be not as children easily stirred and carried away by every wind of false doctrines of men who through their craftiness are artful in deceiving the people;
4.15: But that we be sincere in our love, so that in everything we may progress through Christ, who is the head.
4.16: It is through him that the whole body is closely and firmly united at all joints, according to the measure of the gift which is given to every member, for the guidance and control of the body, in order to complete the edifying of the body in love.
4.17: This I say therefore and testify in the LORD: that you henceforth live not as other Gentiles, who live in the vanity of their mind,
4.18: And whose understanding is dark, and who are alienated from the life of God, because they have no knowledge, and because of the blindness of their hearts;
4.19: And who have given up their hope, and have surrendered themselves to wantonness, and to the practice of all uncleanness in their covetousness.
4.20: But that is not what you have been taught about Christ;
4.21: If you have truly heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is found in Jesus:
4.22: That you lay aside all your former practices, that is to say, the old man which is degenerated with deceitful lusts;
4.23: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;
4.24: And that you put on the new man, who is created by God in righteousness and true holiness.
4.25: Wherefore you must put away from you lying, and speak the truth every man with his neighbor: for we are members one of another.
4.26: Be angry, but sin not: and let not the sun go down upon your anger;
4.27: And do not give the devil a chance.
4.28: From henceforth let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labor with his hands and do good deeds, that he may have something to give to him who is in need.
4.29: Let no bad word proceed from your mouth, but words that are good and useful for edification, that they will impart blessing to those who hear them.
4.30: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, whereby you are sealed unto the day of salvation.
4.31: Let all bitterness and wrath, and anger, and clamouring and blasphemy be put away from you, together with all malice:
4.32: And be kind one to another and tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God has forgiven us through Christ.

5.1: BE therefore God-like, as beloved chil dren.
5.2: And walk in love, as Christ also has loved us and has given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweet example.
5.3: But let not immorality or any uncleanness or covetousness be heard of among you, as becomes saints;
5.4: Neither cursing, nor foolish words, nor insults, nor words of flattery, none of which are necessary: but instead of these, let thanks be offered.
5.5: You should know this: that no one guilty of fornication, or unclean person, or covetous man who serves idols, has any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.
5.6: Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things the anger of God comes on the children of disobedience.
5.7: Therefore do not be partakers with them.
5.8: For previously you were ignorant, but now you have been enlightened by our LORD, and should live therefore like children of light.
5.9: For the fruits of light are found in all goodness, and righteousness, and truth;
5.10: And so you must discern that which is acceptable before our LORD.
5.11: Have no part in the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather condemn them.
5.12: For it is a shame even to speak of the things that are done by them in secret.
5.13: For all things that are condemned are exposed by the light: and anything that is made manifest is light.
5.14: Therefore it is said: Awake thou that sleepest, and rise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.
5.15: Watch therefore, that you live a glorious life, not as foolish men, but as wise men,
5.16: Who take advantage of their opportunity, for these are difficult days.
5.17: Wherefore do not lack wisdom, but understand what the will of God is.
5.18: And do not become drunk with wine, wherein is intemperance; but be filled with the Spirit,
5.19: Speaking to your souls in psalms and hymns, and in spiritual songs, sing with your heart to the LORD;
5.20: Giving thanks always for all men to God the Father in the name of our LORD Jesus Christ.
5.21: Submit yourselves one to another in the love of Christ.
5.22: Wives submit yourselves to your husbands as to our LORD.
5.23: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of its body.
5.24: Therefore as the church is subject to Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.
5.25: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ loved his church, and gave himself for it;
5.26: That he might sanctify and cleanse it, by the washing of water and by the word,
5.27: In order to build for himself a glorious church, without stain or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.
5.28: So should men love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
5.29: For no man ever yet hated his own body; but nourishes it and cherishes it, even as Christ does for his church.
5.30: For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.
5.31: For this reason shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined to his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.
5.32: This is a great mystery; but I speak concerning Christ and his church.
5.33: Nevertheless, let every one of you so love his wife as himself, and the wife see that she reverence her husband.

6.1: CHILDREN, obey your parents in our LORD, for this is right.
6.2: This is the first commandment with promise: Honour thy father and mother;
6.3: That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.
6.4: And parents, do not provoke your children to anger; but bring them up in the discipline and teaching of our LORD.
6.5: Servants, be obedient to your masters according to the flesh, with reverence and trembling, and with a sincere heart, as to Christ;
6.6: Not with eyeservice, as men-pleasers, but as the servants of Christ doing the will of God from the heart.
6.7: And serve well with your whole soul, with love, as to our LORD, and not to men:
6.8: Knowing that whatever good thing any man does, the same shall he receive from our LORD, whether he be a slave or a freeman.
6.9: Also, masters, do the same things for your servants, forgiving their faults, because you also have your own Master in heaven; and there is no respect of persons with him.
6.10: From henceforth, my brethren, be strong in our LORD, and in the power of his might.
6.11: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
6.12: For your conflict is not only with flesh and blood, but also with the angels, and with powers, with the rulers of this world of darkness, and with the evil spirits under the heavens.
6.13: Wherefore put you on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to meet the evil one, and being prepared you shall prevail.
6.14: Arise, therefore, gird your loins with truth, and put on the breastplate of righteousness;
6.15: And have your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace;
6.16: Together with these, take for yourselves the shield of faith, for with it you shall be able to quench all the flaming darts of the wicked.
6.17: And put on the helmet of salvation, and take the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:
6.18: And pray always, with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and in that prayer be watchful at all times, praying constantly and supplicating for all the saints,
6.19: And for me also, that words may be given to me as soon as I open my mouth, so that I may boldly preach the mystery of the gospel,
6.20: For which I am a messenger in chains: that I may speak openly about it, as I ought to speak.
6.21: In order that you also may know my affairs, and what I do, Tychicus, a beloved brother and a faithful minister in our LORD, shall make known to you all things;
6.22: Him I have sent to you for the same purpose, that you may know how I am, and that he may comfort your hearts.
6.23: Peace be to our brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father, and from our LORD Jesus Christ.
6.24: Grace be with all them that love our LORD Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.
